asset_correction.reason: DEPLOY359 code-based lock: Explicit piping code reference (ASME B31.3 or API 570) with piping term.

hotspots: Corrosion + cyclic stress at same location
warnings: Corrosion creates surface pits that become stress risers, accelerating fatigue crack initiation. Combined damage rate is faster than either mechanism alone.
Cyclic stress concentrates at geometric discontinuities (welds_implied_for_piping). These are the most likely crack initiation sites.
Corrosion preferentially attacks areas of high stress and geometric change. Weld toes, crevices, and transitions are most vulnerable.
H2S charges hydrogen into material under tensile stress. This can cause sudden cracking with little warning. High-priority inspection zone.

validated_mechanisms.name: Mechanical Fatigue
validated_mechanisms.weld_context: Fatigue life at weld defect zone is 5-10x shorter than base metal. Incomplete penetration at root creates stress riser for fatigue initiation. DNV-RP-C203 and BS 7910 provide weld-specific fatigue classification.

validated_mechanisms.name: Sulfide Stress Cracking
validated_mechanisms.weld_context: Weld present in sour service -- HAZ and weld root are preferential SSC/HIC initiation sites. Verify hardness per NACE MR0175.

validated_mechanisms.name: Hydrogen Induced Cracking
validated_mechanisms.weld_context: Weld present in sour service -- HAZ and weld root are preferential SSC/HIC initiation sites. Verify hardness per NACE MR0175.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Chloride Stress Corrosion Cracking
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Chloride SCC requires an austenitic or duplex stainless steel substrate. Carbon steel and low-alloy steel are not susceptible to the chloride SCC mechanism.

rejected_mechanisms.name: High-Temperature Sulfidation
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Sulfidation kinetics are negligible below approximately 500F. Above 500F, the corrosion rate increases exponentially with temperature per the McConomy/API 939-C curve.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Thermal Fatigue
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Thermal fatigue requires thermal cycling and stress concentration.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Vibration Fatigue
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Vibration fatigue requires vibration and stress concentration.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Chloride SCC
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: CSCC requires austenitic/duplex stainless.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Caustic SCC
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Caustic SCC requires caustic environment.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Creep Damage
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Creep requires elevated temperature.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Brittle Fracture
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Brittle fracture requires cryogenic temperature.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Mechanical Overload / Buckling
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Overload requires compressive stress or impact. Mechanical Overload / Buckling requires impact energy or overload event; no such event in history.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Fire / Thermal Damage
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Fire damage requires fire exposure.

rejected_mechanisms.name: High Temp Hydrogen Attack
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Hydrogen attack requires temperature > 400F.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Naphthenic Acid Corrosion
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Naphthenic acid corrosion is active in the 400-800F range (peak 500-750F); operating temperature is outside this window. Naphthenic acid corrosion requires naphthenic acid / high TAN environment; transcript does not indicate naphthenic acid service.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Polythionic Acid SCC
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Polythionic acid SCC requires sensitized austenitic or duplex stainless steel. Polythionic acid SCC requires polythionic acid environment (sulfide scale + moisture + oxygen during shutdown); transcript does not indicate polythionic acid cracking context.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Amine SCC
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Amine SCC requires amine service environment (MEA, DEA, MDEA); transcript does not indicate amine service.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Carbonate SCC
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Carbonate SCC requires carbonate/alkaline sour water environment; transcript does not indicate carbonate cracking context.

rejected_mechanisms.name: 885F Embrittlement
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: 885F embrittlement affects ferritic stainless (400-series), martensitic stainless, and the ferrite phase of duplex stainless. Austenitic stainless and carbon steel are not susceptible. 885F embrittlement is active in the 600-1000F range; operating temperature is outside this window.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Sigma Phase Embrittlement
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Sigma phase embrittlement affects austenitic stainless (300-series) and duplex stainless steels. Carbon steel and low-alloy steel are not susceptible. Sigma phase formation requires prolonged exposure in the 1000-1600F range; operating temperature is outside this window.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Temper Embrittlement
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Temper embrittlement primarily affects Cr-Mo low-alloy steels (1Cr through 3Cr); carbon steel and stainless steel are not typically susceptible. Temper embrittlement occurs from service in or slow cooling through the 650-1070F range; operating temperature is outside this window.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Carburization
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Carburization requires temperatures above 1100F; operating temperature is below this threshold.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Metal Dusting
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Metal dusting is active in the 900-1500F range; operating temperature is outside this window.

rejected_mechanisms.name: High-Temp Oxidation
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Significant oxidation scaling requires temperatures above 1000F; operating temperature is below this threshold.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Spheroidization
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Spheroidization requires prolonged exposure above 850F; operating temperature is below this threshold.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Decarburization
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Decarburization requires temperatures above 800F; operating temperature is below this threshold.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Graphitization
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Graphitization requires prolonged exposure above 800F; operating temperature is below this threshold.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Galvanic Corrosion
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Galvanic corrosion requires dissimilar metal contact with an electrolyte present.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Atmospheric Corrosion
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Atmospheric corrosion requires outdoor/weather exposure; no atmospheric exposure language detected.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Soil-Side Corrosion
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Soil-side corrosion requires buried or soil-contact conditions; no buried/soil language detected.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Cavitation Damage
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Cavitation requires evidence of vapor bubble collapse conditions (pumps, valves, restrictions); no cavitation language detected.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Ammonia SCC
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Ammonia SCC requires ammonia environment; no ammonia service language detected.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Hydrogen Embrittlement
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Hydrogen embrittlement requires a hydrogen charging source (cathodic protection, electroplating, pickling, welding, or H2 service); no hydrogen charging language detected.

rejected_mechanisms.name: Wet H2S Blistering/SOHIC
rejected_mechanisms.rejection_reason: CATALOG REJECTED: Wet H2S blistering requires wet (aqueous) H2S service; no wet H2S / sour water language detected.

primary_mechanism.name: Mechanical Fatigue
primary_mechanism.weld_context: Fatigue life at weld defect zone is 5-10x shorter than base metal. Incomplete penetration at root creates stress riser for fatigue initiation. DNV-RP-C203 and BS 7910 provide weld-specific fatigue classification.

damage_reality.physics_narrative: Primary: Mechanical Fatigue (score 0.05). Physics: Cyclic loading + Stress concentration. 3 possible, 31 physically impossible.

consequence_reality.failure_physics: Fatigue crack propagation per Paris Law. Cyclic stress drives incremental growth at stress concentrations. Critical crack size determined by fracture toughness vs applied stress. Failure mode: leak-before-break (ductile) or catastrophic burst (insufficient toughness).
consequence_basis: PHYSICS: Stored pressure energy -- release on failure
PHYSICS: Primary load-carrying member -- collapse risk
PHYSICS: Toxic substance (implied_corrosive, H2S, hydrogen, HTHA_susceptible_material, exchanger_service)
DEPLOY180: 1 impact dimension(s) UNDETERMINED on HIGH asset -- fail-upward applied
consequence_reality.operational_impact: UNDETERMINED -- insufficient evidence to classify operational impact
consequence_reality.damage_trajectory: Active damage mechanism present with gradual progression. Standard monitoring intervals should be maintained or tightened.
threshold_reasons: Multiple damage forces are interacting and amplifying each other at this location.
Active mechanism (Mechanical Fatigue) has threshold behavior -- stable until critical size, then rapid failure.
Note: damage state is based on physics potential, not confirmed observations. Inspection needed to verify actual condition.

counterfactual_challenge.what_if_wrong: If the indication is actually corrosion pitting or geometric artifact rather than fatigue crack, the urgency and failure mode change significantly.
counterfactual_challenge.fastest_resolution_test: Surface preparation + PT/MT for crack confirmation. UT thickness grid to check for competing wall loss. If crack confirmed, TOFD/PAUT for depth sizing.
counterfactual_challenge.decision_critical_question: Is the observed damage consistent with Mechanical Fatigue, or could a competing mechanism (Sulfide Stress Cracking) better explain the findings?
